sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data available for this boxing game

special_mechanics.name: Special Attacks
special_mechanics.description: Comical special attacks activated by pressing attack button at correct timing and distance. Three main types: spinning hook, straight punch that propels opponent into ropes, and uppercut that launches opponent into air (can result in ring-out KO if timed correctly)

physics_values.notes: Being countered before special attack lands causes extraordinary damage

special_mechanics.name: Stamina System
special_mechanics.description: Boxer stamina deplenishes with missed punches and excessive attacks. Stamina recovers during round intervals by rapid button pressing

special_mechanics.name: Counter-Punching
special_mechanics.description: Defensive mechanic allowing boxers to counter-punch opponent attacks

tier_notes: Tier 3: Ring King is a boxing game where precise physics movement data is not publicly documented. Available sources provide gameplay mechanics and special attack descriptions but no quantitative physics parameters. As a beat-em-up/boxing title, it uses stamina management and special attack timing rather than detailed platformer physics. No sub-pixel rendering, gravity, jump mechanics, or movement speeds are documented in available sources.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Ring King (NES) article
